methodsAttempted.query: site:ecu.edu.au Edith Cowan University generative AI ChatGPT policy academic integrity students
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found ECU research repository articles and a public ECU student news item on using AI appropriately.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ecu.edu.au AI at uni Generative AI Edith Cowan University
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search confirmed the official ECU student news item and a student news listing.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ecu.edu.au Artificial Intelligence academic integrity ECU policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official Generative AI in Learning page, AI Framework page, academic-integrity pages, and research repository results.

Official ECU pages were fetched with Firecrawl. Direct curl attempts against www.ecu.edu.au and intranet.ecu.edu.au returned a Cloudflare challenge, so direct HTTP was not used to bypass that restriction.

methodsAttempted.query: ECU generative AI acknowledgement referencing academic misconduct
methodsAttempted.notes: The official ECU Library LibGuide provided public guidance on acknowledging and referencing generative AI use.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found ECU's public Artificial Intelligence Framework, student-facing Generative AI in Learning page, student news guidance on using AI appropriately, ECU Library generative-AI acknowledgement guidance, and academic-integrity pages. Canvas content was login-gated, two guessed generic paths were 404s, and research repository articles were rejected as research outputs rather than ECU policy or guidance.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:ecu.edu.au Artificial Intelligence academic integrity ECU policy
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official ECU Centre for Learning and Teaching page describing ECU's AI framework, ethical principles, responsible-use guidelines, oversight, advisors, and knowledge base.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:ecu.edu.au Artificial Intelligence academic integrity ECU policy
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official ECU Student Intranet page outlining public student support for responsible generative-AI use, attribution, prompt strategies, drop-ins, and workshops.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI at uni: how to use it the right way
artifacts.queryUsed: site:ecu.edu.au Edith Cowan University generative AI ChatGPT policy academic integrity students
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official ECU student news guidance explains how existing academic-integrity rules apply to generative AI and gives student-facing use boundaries.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI and Copilot: 6. Acknowledgement & Referencing
artifacts.queryUsed: ECU generative AI acknowledgement referencing academic misconduct
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official ECU Library LibGuide page gives student guidance on acknowledging and referencing generative AI and links it to academic misconduct.

Official ECU academic-integrity hub defines academic integrity and lists the core academic-integrity rules, policy, and procedures. It is used as contextual support, not as an AI-specific claim by itself.
Official ECU academic-misconduct page explicitly states that plagiarism includes unacknowledged use of a generative artificial intelligence tool such as ChatGPT.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using Generative AI Tools in Learning
artifacts.verificationNotes: The official Canvas page linked from ECU's public student GenAI page redirected to Microsoft sign-in. It was not accessed or used as evidence.
Firecrawl returned ECU's Page Not Found content for this guessed generic policies path.
Firecrawl returned ECU's Page Not Found content for this older Academic Skills Centre path.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI at uni: how to use it the right way
Generative AI and Copilot: 6. Acknowledgement & Referencing

artifacts.claimText: Edith Cowan University has a public Artificial Intelligence Framework intended to empower staff and students to use AI productively and ethically, with ethical principles and responsible-use guidance rather than a standalone prohibition.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: ECU provides student-facing generative-AI learning support that covers responsible use, learning, academic integrity, prompt strategies, attribution, drop-ins, and workshops.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: ECU tells students that the same academic-integrity rules that apply to traditional study also apply to generative AI, and that submitting AI-generated work without permission is an academic-integrity breach.

citation.citationTitle: AI at uni: how to use it the right way
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: ECU Library guidance says students who use generative AI as a study or research tool must acknowledge that use in their assessment, and that not giving credit to GenAI tools counts as plagiarism.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: ECU's academic-misconduct guidance states that plagiarism includes unacknowledged use of a generative artificial intelligence tool such as ChatGPT.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The purpose of this framework is to empower and enable staff and students to productively and ethically use AI, in line with ECU's vision to lead the sector in the educational experience, research with impact, and in positive contributions to industry and communities.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Main Content > More about this framework
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Productive use will be primarily supported by practical Guidelines for Responsible Use. They articulate the application of the Ethical Principles to different domains of work, which at this stage are: Learning, Teaching and Student Support; Research and Research Training; Organisational Productivity.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Main Content > Elements of the framework > Guidelines for Responsible Use
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: ECU students can access a range of resources on Canvas that offer guidance on using these tools. In alignment with ECU's Ethical Framework, these resources include: advice on how to use gen AI for learning and demonstrating academic integrity; practical prompt strategies for obtaining meaningful feedback; guides for correctly attributing AI-assisted work.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: At ECU, the same rules of academic integrity that apply to traditional study also apply to Generative AI. That means honesty, fairness and doing your own work.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Submitting AI-generated work without permission is an academic integrity breach, just like plagiarism. It's also risky to trust AI to write your content as it may hallucinate information or sources.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI and Copilot: 6. Acknowledgement & Referencing
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If you use generative AI like a tool to help your study and research process (as discussed in this guide), you must still acknowledge the use you have put it to even though it has not been used to directly create assessment output.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI and Copilot: 6. Acknowledgement & Referencing
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Not giving credit to Gen AI tools counts as plagiarism. If you get caught, you could get anything from a warning letter to losing marks, failing the assignment, or even more serious actions like being suspended.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI and Copilot: 6. Acknowledgement & Referencing
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Plagiarism is defined as presenting, intentionally or unintentionally, the ideas or work of another person as one's own ideas, or work without appropriate referencing or acknowledgement. Plagiarism is the most common and well-known form of academic misconduct. It also includes unacknowledged use of a generative artificial intelligence tool such as ChatGPT
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Main Content > Avoiding Academic Misconduct > What is Plagiarism?

artifacts.notes: Claim is scoped to ECU's public framework and avoids treating it as a standalone binding prohibition or permission list.
Claim is limited to public student support and does not rely on login-gated Canvas details.
Claim directly follows the official student news wording and preserves the condition that permission and unit guidance matter.
Claim uses ECU Library wording and is limited to assessment acknowledgement guidance.
Claim is a narrow academic-integrity statement from ECU's Avoiding Academic Misconduct page.

artifacts.title: Edith Cowan University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Official ECU sources show a public AI framework for productive and ethical use, student-facing generative-AI learning support, and academic-integrity guidance requiring permission and acknowledgement for AI-assisted work. Login-gated Canvas guidance was not accessed, and research repository outputs were not used as policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Canvas and SharePoint resources linked by official pages were not accessed because they required login.
Direct curl requests to www.ecu.edu.au and intranet.ecu.edu.au returned a Cloudflare challenge; Firecrawl-accessible official page text was used for staged evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
